what: Interface to the uv Python Package Manager
title: Interface to the uv Python Package Manager
text: Call uv R.

title: Interface to the uv Python Package Manager
Interface to the uv Python Package Manager
code: library(uv)  with_temp_project({   # Initialise a uv project in the current directory   uv::init()      # Create a local virtual environment   uv::venv()      # Add a runtime dependency (declares it in pyproject.toml)   uv::add("requests")      # Resolve and lock dependencies   uv::lock()      # Synchronise the environment with the lockfile   uv::sync()      # Run uv commands directly if needed   # same as `uv::version()   uv("--version")      # Run a tool in an ephemeral environment   uvx("pycowsay", "hello from uv") }) #>  #> ── Initialising Python project #> ✔ Writing main.py (<file:///tmp/RtmpSg4QMs/uv-project-3671034c59d5ec/main.py>) #> ✔ Writing pyproject.toml #>   (<file:///tmp/RtmpSg4QMs/uv-project-3671034c59d5ec/pyproject.toml>) #> ✔ Writing README.md #>   (<file:///tmp/RtmpSg4QMs/uv-project-3671034c59d5ec/README.md>) #> Using CPython 3.11.13 #> Creating virtual environment at: .venv #> Activate with: source .venv/bin/activate #>  #> ── Adding packages #> ℹ Updating pyproject.toml (<file:///tmp/RtmpSg4QMs/uv-project-3671034c59d5ec/pyproject.toml>) #> ✔ certifi==2026.1.4 #> ✔ charset-normalizer==3.4.4 #> ✔ idna==3.11 #> ✔ requests==2.32.5 #> ✔ urllib3==2.6.3 #> ! warning: Failed to hardlink files; falling back to full copy. This may lead to degraded performance. #> ✔ Lockfile updated. #> Resolved 6 packages in 0.33ms #> Audited 5 packages in 0.00ms #> uv 0.7.13 (62ed17b23 2025-06-12) #>  #>   ------------- #> < hello from uv > #>   ------------- #>    \   ^__^ #>     \  (oo)\_______ #>        (__)\       )\/\ #>            ||----w | #>            ||     ||

text: check_up_to_date Logical; TRUE, check whether lockfile consistent pyproject.toml without modifying . check_exists Logical; TRUE, assert lockfile exists. upgrade Logical; TRUE, upgrade locked packages. upgrade_package Optional package name (pkg==version) upgrade. path Directory inside uv project. verbose Whether chatty.

title: Resolve and validate project dependencies — lock

what: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
title: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
text: High-level interface uv command-line tool. function provides structured API uv's command grammar, separating uv-level arguments R-level control parameters. uv commands follow general form:   grammar made explicit via namespace, verb, .args, .flags parameters. R-level control parameters prefixed . clearly distinguish arguments passed uv.

title: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
code: uv(   verb = "help",   namespace = NULL,   .args = character(),   .flags = character(),   .path = ".",   .require_project = FALSE,   .require_pyproject = FALSE,   .require_lock = FALSE,   .echo = TRUE,   .error_on_status = FALSE,   .dry_run = FALSE )

title: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
text: verb single character string giving uv subcommand execute (e.g. "sync", "install", "find"). namespace Optional command namespace (e.g. "pip", "python", "tool"). NULL, top-level uv command invoked. .args Character vector positional arguments passed uv. .flags Character vector option flags passed uv (e.g. "--upgrade", "--group", "dev"). .path Directory used resolve uv project root. .require_project Logical; TRUE, requires pyproject.toml uv.lock present. .require_pyproject Logical; TRUE, requires pyproject.toml present. Use commands can run lockfile exists. .require_lock Logical; whether command requires existing uv.lock file. .echo Logical; whether stream uv output console. .error_on_status Logical; whether error uv exits non-zero status code. .dry_run Logical; TRUE, return constructed command without executing .

title: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
text: processx result object, (.dry_run = TRUE) constructed command character vector class "uv_command".

title: Call uv using structured command semantics — uv
code: with_temp_project({    # 1. Initialise project   uv("init")   fs::file_info("pyproject.toml")    # 2. Declare a dependency   uv("add", .args = "requests")   readLines("pyproject.toml")    # 3. Resolve and lock dependencies   uv("lock")   fs::file_info("uv.lock")    # 4. Materialise environment   uv("sync", .require_lock = TRUE)   fs::dir_ls(".venv")    # 5. Prove the package is importable   uv("run", .args = c("python", "-c", "import requests; print(requests.__version__)")) }) #> Error: ! command is not a string (length 1 character)

title: Execute the uv binary — uv_exec
text: processx result object.

title: Execute the uv binary — uv_exec

title: Lock project dependencies using uv (engine) — uv_lock
text: check_up_to_date Logical; TRUE, check whether uv.lock consistent pyproject.toml without modifying . check_exists Logical; TRUE, assert uv.lock file exists without checking whether --date. upgrade Logical; TRUE, upgrade locked packages latest versions allowed project constraints. upgrade_package Optional package name (pkg==version) upgrade leaving locked versions unchanged. script Optional path Python script lock instead project. .path Directory inside uv project. .dry_run Logical; TRUE, return command without executing .

title: Lock project dependencies using uv (engine) — uv_lock
text: processx result object, uv_command .dry_run = TRUE.

title: Lock project dependencies using uv (engine) — uv_lock

title: Locate the uv executable — uv_path
text: Determines path uv executable checking fixed explicit sequence locations.

title: Locate the uv executable — uv_path
Locate the uv executable — uv_path
text: character string giving path uv executable, NULL uv located.

title: Locate the uv executable — uv_path
text: lookup performed following order: UV environment variable set non-empty, value used path uv. Otherwise, system PATH searched using Sys.("uv"). uv still found, following OS-specific fallback locations checked, order: Windows: %USERPROFILE%\\.local\\bin\\uv.exe %LOCALAPPDATA%\\uv\\uv.exe %USERPROFILE%\\.cargo\\bin\\uv.exe macOS Unix-like systems: ~/.local/bin/uv ~/.cargo/bin/uv none checks succeed, NULL returned. Setting UV environment variable allows users explicitly control uv executable used. useful multiple installations present uv installed non-standard location system PATH.

title: Locate the uv executable — uv_path
